wikitext:
{{Sequel Disambiguation|2}}
{{2|Orbs}} are a mechanic used by {{KW|Defect|The Defect|2}}. They operate identical to their Slay the Spire origins, with the addition of a new Orb type.

== Mechanics ==
'''Orbs''' have two abilities each, a '''Passive''' effect that happens every turn, and an '''Evoke''' effect.

=== Orb Slots ===
Using Orbs requires a player have '''Orb Slots'''. Each slot can hold one Orb.

Orb Slots follow a first-in, first-out structure. Visually, Orbs are both Channeled and Evoked in right-to left order, with Orbs shifting right to fill any empty slots.

The {{2|Defect}} will start combat with 3 Orb Slots. Gaining a Orb as any character without Orb Slots will add 1 Orb Slot.

You can have a maximum of 10 Orb Slots at once.

=== Channel ===
When an Orb is '''Channeled''', it is placed into the right-most available Orb Slot. If all Orb Slots are occupied, the right-most Orb is Evoked, making space for the newly Channeled Orb.

=== Evoke ===
When an Orb is '''Evoked''', it is removed from its Slot (allowing Orbs behind it to move forward), and its Evoke effect is activated.

=== Passive ===
Each Orb type has a '''Passive''' ability which is activated automatically once per turn. {{KW|Lightning||2}}, {{KW|Frost||2}}, {{KW|Dark||2}}, and {{KW|Glass||2}} Orbs activate their Passives at end of turn, while {{KW|Plasma||2}} Orbs activate theirs at the beginning of your turn.

== Orb Types ==
There are five types of Orbs.

=== Lightning Orbs ===
{{KW|Lightning|Lightning Orbs|2}} are the most common damage dealing Orbs. Their Passive effect deals 3 damage to a random enemy at end of turn. When Evoked, they instead do 8 damage to a random enemy.

Lightning is the most common Orb type, having the most cards which Channel it, as well as being generated by both the Defect's starter relics, {{R|Cracked Core||2}} and {{R|Infused Core||2}}, and one of its Basics, {{C|Zap||2}}. It also has the most support of the Orb types, with {{C|Voltaic||2}} scaling based on how many Lightning you've Channeled this combat.

=== Frost Orbs ===
{{KW|Frost|Frost Orbs|2}} are the {{KW|Block||2}} generating Orbs. Their Passive effect gives 2 {{KW|Block}} at end of turn, while the Evoke effect gives 5 {{KW|Block||2}}.

Frost is only Orb type to not gain any exclusive Sources from Slay the Spire, becoming more rare after losing one exclusive source: the {{R|Frozen Core}}.

=== Dark Orbs ===
{{KW|Dark|Dark Orbs|2}} are another type of damage dealing Orbs, with an emphasis on scaling. Dark Orbs have a damage value, which starts at 6 when Channeled. Their Passive adds 6 to their damage value at end of turn, while their Evoke ability deals the damage value to the enemy with the lowest current HP.

If multiple enemies have the lowest HP, the left-most enemy is chosen first.

Dark Orbs gained availability from Slay the Spire. They generally require a different approach. While the other Orb types have similar effects for their Passive and Evoked abilities, Dark Orbs do very little if Evoked before applying their Passive, and benefit from their Passive ability being used before Evoking.

=== Glass Orbs ===
{{KW|Glass|Glass Orbs|2}} are a new Orb introduced in Slay the Spire 2. When Channeled, Glass Orbs start with an initial damage value of 4. Their Passive deals their current damage value to all enemies at end of turn, then decreases their damage value by 1, while their Evoke effect deals twice their current damage value to all enemies.

Glass orbs are a rare orb type with limited sources, with a focus on high throughput Channeling and Evoking, contrasting with {{KW|Dark|Dark Orbs|2}}. Because of their Passive damage decrease, if enough turns pass after a Glass Orb is Channeled, its damage value will eventually reach 0.

=== Plasma Orbs ===
{{KW|Plasma|Plasma Orbs|2}} are the {{Icon|DE|2}} Energy generating Orbs. Their Passive generates 1 {{Icon|DE|2}} at the start of the turn, while Evoking them generates 2 {{Icon|DE|2}} instead. Unlike the other Orb types, Plasma Orbs are wholly unaffected by {{BD|Focus||2}}.

Plasma orbs are decidedly the least common Orb type. Given how differently they work from the other Orb types, they are able to synergize with things the other Orbs do not, perhaps due to reducing {{BD|Focus||2}}.

== Orb Sources ==
=== Lightning ===
* Cards: {{C|Zap||2}}, {{C|Ball Lightning||2}}, {{C|Lightning Rod||2}}, {{C|Chaos||2}}, {{C|Storm||2}}, {{C|Tempest||2}}, {{C|Rainbow||2}}, {{C|Trash to Treasure||2}}
* Relic: {{R|Cracked Core}}, {{R|Infused Core|}}

=== Frost ===
* Cards: {{C|Cold Snap||2}}, {{C|Coolheaded||2}}, {{C|Chill||2}}, {{C|Chaos||2}}, {{C|Glacier||2}}, {{C|Rainbow||2}}, {{C|Trash to Treasure||2}}

=== Dark ===
* Cards: {{C|Darkness||2}}, {{C|Chaos||2}}, {{C|Null||2}}, {{C|Shadow Shield||2}}, {{C|Consuming Shadow||2}}, {{C|Rainbow||2}}, {{C|Trash to Treasure||2}}
* Relic: {{R|Symbiotic Virus||2}}
* Potion: {{P|Essence of Darkness||2}}

=== Plasma ===
* Cards: {{C|Fusion||2}}, {{C|Chaos||2}}, {{C|Ignition||2}}, {{C|Meteor Strike||2}}, {{C|Trash to Treasure||2}}

=== Glass ===
* Cards: {{C|Glasswork||2}}, {{C|Refract||2}}, {{C|Chaos||2}}, {{C|Spinner||2}}, {{C|Trash to Treasure||2}}

=== Orb Slots ===
* Cards: {{C|Capacitor||2}}, {{C|Modded||2}}, {{C|Bulk Up||2}} (Lose Orb Slot)
* Relics: {{R|Runic Capacitor||2}}
* Potion: {{P|Potion of Capacity||2}}

== Interactions ==
=== Cards that Evoke Orbs ===
* {{C|Dualcast||2}}: Evoke the rightmost Orb twice.
* {{C|Quadcast||2}}: Evoke the rightmost Orb four times.
* {{C|Consuming Shadow||2}}: At the end of your turn, Evoke the leftmost Orb.
* {{C|Multi-Cast||2}}: Evoke the rightmost Orb X(+1) times.
* {{C|Shatter||2}}: Evoke all of your Orbs.

=== Benefits from Channeled Orbs ===
* {{C|Barrage||2}}: Deal 5(7) damage for each Channeled Orb.
* {{C|Compile Driver||2}}: Draw 1 card for each unique Orb you have.
* {{C|Synchronize||2}}: Gain 2 {{BD|Focus||2}} this turn for each unique Orb you have.
* {{C|Coolant||2}}: At the start of your turn, gain 2(3) {{KW|Block||2}} for each unique Orb you have.
* {{C|Hailstorm||2}}: At the end of your turn, if you have {{KW|Frost||2}}, deal 6(8) damage to ALL enemies.
* {{C|Voltaic||2}}: Channel {{KW|Lightning||2}} equal to the {{KW|Lightning||2}} already Channeled this combat.
* {{R|Metronome||2}}: The first time you Channel 7 Orbs each combat, deal 30 damage to ALL enemies.

=== Extra Passives ===
* {{R|Emotion Chip||2}}: If you lost HP during the previous turn, trigger the passive ability of all Orbs at the start of your turn.
* {{R|Gold-Plated Cables||2}}: Your rightmost Orb triggers its passive an additional time.
* {{C|Loop||2}}: At the start of your turn, trigger the passive ability of your rightmost Orb (twice).
* {{C|Darkness||2}}: Trigger the passive ability of all {{KW|Dark||2}} Orbs (twice).

=== Evoke Effect ===
* {{C|Thunder||2}}: Whenever you Evoke {{KW|Lightning||2}}, deal 6(8) damage to each enemy hit.

== Focus ==
{{Power Infobox|Focus|2}}
{{BD|Focus||2}} is a [[Buff]] that improves the power of your non-{{KW|Plasma||2}} Orbs. It affects the values listed above for {{KW|Lightning||2}}, {{KW|Frost||2}}, {{KW|Dark||2}}, and {{KW|Glass||2}} Orbs, except for the base damage value for {{KW|Dark||2}} Orbs, which is always 6. Functioning very similarly to {{KW|Strength||2}} and {{KW|Dexterity||2}}, Focus may be positive or negative, acting as an offset for the values in the appropriate direction, to a minimum of 0.

As Focus can improve both your damage and {{KW|Block|Blocking|2}} capabilities, it may serve as extremely potent scaling.

=== Sources ===
==== Positive Focus ====
* Cards: Temporary ({{C|Focused Strike||2}}, {{C|Hotfix||2}}, {{C|Synchronize||2}}), {{C|Defragment||2}}, {{C|Biased Cognition||2}}
* Relic: {{R|Data Disk||2}}
* Potion: {{P|Focus Potion||2}}

==== Negative Focus ====
* Cards: {{C|Hyperbeam||2}}
* Debuffs: {{BD|Biased Cognition||2}}
